fault_hypothesis: Lines 74-75 incorrectly remove one copy of the last element when both streams end with the same value, but the spec requires both copies to be preserved. The entire if/pop block is the bug.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true when both streams share a last element that must appear twice in the output.
After popping, the merged list should still contain at least as many elements as the union requires (both copies of shared elements).
The filtered merged result must contain at least as many elements as the longer of the two input streams.
After each loop iteration, the merged list length should equal the sum of consumed elements from both streams.
